text.title: Guardrails Implementation Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to wire a GuardrailsPipeline class into a production LLM app.

step.name: define-config
step.action: Author a `GuardrailConfig` dataclass with enable_pii_detection, enable_prompt_injection_detection, enable_content_moderation, enable_hallucination_detection, moderation_cutoff, async_fanout. Load from YAML at startup.

step.name: wire-input-pipeline
step.action: Compose ordered_checks cheap-first: regex_injection → length_cap → api_moderation → llm_classifier. Each returns Violation or None. Return (sanitized_input, [violations]). Block on injection or moderation hit.
step.decision-gate: If injection or moderation hit → return None (blocked); do not run subsequent checks.

step.name: wire-output-pipeline
step.action: Run validators (schema_check, pii_check, optionally hallucination_check w/ context). On accept, apply filters (pii_redact, etc.). On reject, return Violation.

step.name: emit-violation-log
step.action: POST {trace_id, layer, violation_type, severity, decision, latency_ms} to violation_log_sink. Use a typed sink (HTTP or local file → forwarder).

step.name: optional-async-fanout
step.action: Wrap independent checks in ThreadPoolExecutor / asyncio.gather. Aggregate results; respect block-first semantics.
step.decision-gate: If async_fanout=true but checks depend on each other → revert to sequential.
